castle_v1.palette: heavy stone / fortified (Retro Fantasy fortified + tower family)
castle_v1.target_silhouette: visual anchor of the kingdom: central gate front + two back towers + heavy walls on flanks; courtyard okay (no interior roof).
castle_v1.anchors: 1-unit snap; prefab origin = center. For 3x3 footprint, tile centers are at x,z in {-1, 0, 1}.
recipe_skeleton_pieces: Models/GLB format/tower-top.glb                  (on each back tower, lift Y by 1 unit = 4x ])
Optional extras (still <=20): wall-fortified-paint-window.glb on flanks, roof-high-side.glb + roof-high-side-corner.glb pitched across back, wall-fortified-paint-door.glb side entrances.

warrior_guild_v1.palette: painted plaster body (wall-paint-*) + imposing roofline (roof-high-side) — 'armory feel'
warrior_guild_v1.target_silhouette: compact square body, pitched roof, one gate, flanks with window slits — distinct from peasant house via (a) roof-high-side instead of roof-side, (b) wall-paint family instead of wall-pane-painted-wood.
warrior_guild_v1.anchors: 2x2 footprint — tile centers at x,z in {-0.5, 0.5}.
recipe_skeleton_pieces: Optional: Models/GLB format/wall-detail.glb detail; Models/GLB format/floor-flat.glb base
warrior_guild_v1.kit_family_note: This is the REFERENCE kit for Ranger/Rogue. Save piece positions; Ranger/Rogue will reuse XZ layout with material-family swap.

ranger_guild_v1.palette: plain wood walls (wall-pane-wood-*) + standard pitched roof (roof-side) + optional Nature Kit tree accent
ranger_guild_v1.target_silhouette: hunter's lodge: same body shape as Warrior but wood-tone walls, less imposing roof, one green accent.
recipe_skeleton_pieces: Optional Nature Kit accent: Models/GLTF format/tree_pineSmallB.glb placed adjacent to the front doorway (exercises factor-only shader path).

rogue_guild_v1.palette: bare stone (wall.glb, wall-half.glb, wall-window.glb) — steel-gray, low-profile, asymmetric
rogue_guild_v1.target_silhouette: lower profile than Warrior/Ranger; use wall-half for short segments; off-center doorway; shallow roof.

wizard_guild_v1_optional.palette: tower motif on a 2x2 base; purple accent via paint-family pieces if any fit.
wizard_guild_v1_optional.target_silhouette: tall vertical punctuation point — one tower stacked centrally, walls wrap the base.
recipe_skeleton_pieces: Models/GLB format/tower-paint.glb           (on top of tower-paint-base, lift another 4x ])
wizard_guild_v1_optional.note: Only attempt if energy remains after the core four. Not a sprint blocker.

validation_i_run_after_each_save: Read assets/prefabs/buildings/<prefab_id>.json; assert prefab_id matches file stem, building_type == expected, footprint_tiles == config.py value, ground_anchor_y == 0.0.
Assert len(pieces) <= piece_budget and every pieces[].model resolves under assets/models/.
Trim coarse auto-attribution (e.g., drop kenney_survival-kit if no Survival pieces used).
Update assets/ATTRIBUTION.md if a new pack is introduced.

python main.py --renderer ursina                            # after Agent 03 loader lands (no env var)
$env:KINGDOM_URSINA_PREFAB_TEST='0'; python main.py --renderer ursina   # opt-out regression check

wk30_r1_prefabs_landed.flag_for_agent_03: castle_v1 uses front-left-outer anchor convention (xz ∈ [-0.5..1.5] × [0.5..2.5]) rather than center-of-footprint. WK29 peasant house also used this (xz=(-0.5, 0.5) for 1x1). If the generalized loader in wk30 expects center-anchor, the castle will render shifted by (0.5, 1.5) from its building anchor. Either the loader should honor the schema.md 'center-of-footprint' clause and add a translation offset, or schema.md should be amended in a follow-up to match the assembler's actual convention. Non-blocking for this sprint — matches wk29 behavior.

All pass schema + PM acceptance; all use Retro Fantasy GLBs exclusively (textured path — unlit shader), no factor-only pieces, so classifier routes all through the textured path.

r2_fix_screenshot_review.root_cause: PM rejected all 5 POI captures. Diagnosis: the 5 new building types were never added to game/content/buildings.py BUILDING_DEFS (the r1 handoff to agent_05 asked for exactly this registration and it never landed), so Building.size fell back to (1,1). The renderer's prefab footprint fit (ursina_entity_render_collab.sync_prefab_building_entity: scale_x = fx/effective_w, Y untouched) then squeezed every prefab's XZ into ONE tile -> tall skinny clumps (outpost/ruins), 'two brown boxes' (dragon cave cliff pieces), invisible sub-pixel props (well). NOT a path bug: all piece model files existed; control graveyard worked because poi_graveyard IS in BUILDING_DEFS. Two secondary prefab-side bugs: kit's windmill-fantasy-town.glb is BLADES ONLY (no mill body), and the well's rock ring sat inside the fountain rim (verified numerically via GLB POSITION-accessor bboxes: fountain is 2.0 wide x 0.28 tall; wall-broken-fantasy-town is a 0.11-wide end-post sliver).
what_i_changed: game/content/buildings.py — OUT-OF-LANE BUT REQUIRED: added the 5 WK132 POI BuildingDefs (sizes mirror POI_DEFINITIONS: well 1x1, outpost 3x3, windmill 2x2, ruins 5x5, dragon cave 3x3; is_poi=True, cost/occupants 0). Without this no prefab-side change could fix the renders.

poi_mysterious_well_v1.json — fountain XZ 0.42 (rim inside ring) with Y 1.2 raised lip; rock ring moved OUTSIDE rim (radius ~0.5-0.55) and scaled 1.2; lantern 1.6; grass 2.0.

evidence.validate_assets: errors=0 warns=46 (all pre-existing; zero findings mention poi_*)

learned: Prefab piece positions are TILE CENTERS: keep max |pos| <= (footprint-1)/2 or the renderer's fit (effective_w = max(authored, spread+1)) squeezes XZ while leaving Y, distorting everything.
Any new poi_*/building_type MUST be registered in game/content/buildings.py BUILDING_DEFS (and the WK70 snapshot test) or Building.size silently falls back to (1,1).
Verify kit piece extents numerically (GLB POSITION accessors min/max) before layout — several Kenney pieces are slivers or attachment-only (windmill = blades only).

pieces.stall-green-fantasy-town.glb: herald's booth, back-center [1.0,0,1.5] rot 180 (awning faces front)
pieces.signpost.glb (Survival, double): primary 'notices posted here' read, front-left [0.4,0,0.25] scale 1.8
pieces.lantern-fantasy-town.glb: right side [1.8,0,1.2] scale 0.45 (FT pack multiplier makes 1.0 a giant lamppost — see lessons)
pieces.banner-red-fantasy-town.glb: hung on booth's left flank [0.55,0,1.2] rot 90 scale 0.8 — identity pop; Kenney ships no blue banner, royal-blue identity stays in map-icon/UI color

lessons: lantern-fantasy-town.glb at scale 1.0 renders as a full-height street lamppost taller than the stall (FT extent multiplier); 0.45 reads as a small lamp.

banner-red-fantasy-town.glb origin is at its base and the cloth hangs UP from y — placing it at y 0.6 floats it; place at y 0.0 against a tall piece and rotate 90 so the cloth faces the RTS camera (rot 0 is edge-on = thin red pole).
Brown-on-brown: a signpost in front of the stall body disappears; keep wood signs over open grass, offset from the booth silhouette.
Renderer recentres pieces by min/max centroid (_load_prefab_instance), so warrior_guild-style tile-centre coords (0.5/1.5 for 2x2) are fine as long as XZ spread stays within ~2 tiles.
